title: Carbon Tax Impact
description: How have carbon tax policies affected electricity prices for residential consumers in the United States since 2015?
narrative: The user is looking for analyses, reports, or empirical studies that examine the relationship between implemented carbon taxes and changes in residential electricity rates across U.S. states after 2015. Relevant documents should provide data, methodology, and findings on price trends, policy variations, and any confounding factors. Sources that merely mention carbon taxes without quantitative impact on electricity costs, or that focus on non‑U.S. regions, are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks detailed, up-to-date information on financial incentives that support homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems, including federal tax credits, state rebates, utility programs, and financing options. Relevant material must describe eligibility criteria, application procedures, monetary values, deadlines, and any recent legislative changes, and should be specific to the United States. Documents that only discuss solar technology, generic benefits, or non‑U.S. programs are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat islands affect energy consumption in residential buildings during summer months?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, government reports, or credible case studies that quantify the impact of the urban heat island (UHI) effect on residential electricity demand for cooling during the summer. Relevant material should include empirical measurements of temperature differentials between urban and surrounding rural areas, statistical or simulation analyses linking those temperature differences to increased cooling loads, and discussion of mitigation measures (e.g., green roofs, reflective surfaces). Sources that focus on specific cities or provide comparative cross‑city data are especially pertinent, while purely theoretical or non‑energy‑focused discussions of UHI are not considered relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal tax credits and state-level incentives are available for residential solar panel installations in 2024?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date (2024) information on financial incentives for installing solar panels on a home, including the federal Investment Tax Credit, any state or local rebates, grants, tax exemptions, eligibility criteria, monetary amounts, application processes, and deadlines. Relevant documents should describe these programs, compare their benefits, and reference official government or utility sources.

title: Solar Policy Impact
description: What policy mechanisms have proven most effective at accelerating solar power adoption in developing countries?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources—such as peer‑reviewed studies, government or NGO reports, and detailed case studies—that evaluate specific policy instruments (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, tax credits, net‑metering, renewable portfolio standards) and their measurable impact on solar photovoltaic deployment in low‑income or developing nations. Relevant documents must present quantitative results or clear qualitative assessments of adoption rates, discuss implementation challenges or lessons learned, and be published from the year 2000 onward.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What recent policy measures have EU countries introduced to promote residential renewable energy adoption?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on European Union and individual member‑state policies enacted since 2020 that specifically incentivize or mandate renewable energy technologies in homes (e.g., solar photovoltaics, heat pumps, battery storage). Relevant content should describe the policy objectives, financial incentives, regulatory requirements, implementation timelines, and any reported impacts on residential renewable energy uptake. General climate change or energy‑security discussions that do not reference concrete policy actions are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Island
description: What mitigation strategies have proven effective in reducing urban heat island effects in temperate cities?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based measures—such as green roofs, street tree planting, reflective pavements, and related policies—that have demonstrably lowered ambient temperatures in temperate urban environments. Relevant documents should describe specific interventions, provide quantitative temperature reduction data, and discuss implementation challenges and outcomes.

title: Hybrid Workspaces
description: How are companies redesigning office spaces to accommodate hybrid work models while maintaining collaboration and employee well‑being?
narrative: The user is looking for recent (2019‑present) research, industry reports, or case studies that detail architectural, technological, and cultural modifications to office environments that support hybrid work. Relevant material should describe layout changes (e.g., activity‑based working, hot‑desking), reservation and occupancy technologies, air‑quality and health measures, and documented impacts on collaboration, productivity, and employee satisfaction.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What policy mechanisms have proven most effective at accelerating solar and wind power deployment in developing countries?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive overview of government and regulatory approaches—such as feed‑in tariffs, auctions, tax incentives, and grid reforms—that have successfully increased renewable energy capacity in low‑ and middle‑income nations. Relevant documents should describe specific policies, provide quantitative results or case‑study evidence, and discuss implementation challenges and lessons learned. Materials focused solely on technical specifications, unrelated health topics, or bacteriophage biology are not relevant.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal and state tax incentives are currently available for homeowners installing residential solar panels in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on monetary incentives that reduce the cost of installing solar photovoltaic systems on private residences. Relevant documents should detail the federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC) amount, eligibility requirements, and expiration dates, as well as comparable state‑level rebates, tax credits, or performance‑based incentives that apply to residential installations. The user is interested in the monetary value, qualifying criteria (e.g., income limits, system size), application procedures, and any deadlines or phase‑out schedules. Documents that only discuss solar technology, general environmental benefits, or commercial/utility‑scale incentives are not relevant.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal tax credits are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date information on U.S. federal incentives for homeowners who install photovoltaic solar systems, including eligibility requirements, credit percentages or dollar amounts, phase‑out schedules, and guidance on how to claim the credit; relevant sources must cite official IRS or Department of Energy publications, summarize the program’s rules, and provide links to application or filing instructions.

title: Urban Farming Impact
description: What are the environmental, economic, and social impacts of implementing sustainable urban farming practices in mid-sized cities?
narrative: The user wants detailed, evidence‑based information that evaluates how sustainable urban farming affects carbon emissions, local economies, food security, and community cohesion. Relevant documents should be peer‑reviewed studies, government or NGO reports, or well‑documented case studies from cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000, and must address at least two of the specified impact dimensions.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do different green‑infrastructure strategies mitigate urban heat island effects in midsize cities?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative sources—such as peer‑reviewed studies, governmental or NGO reports, and detailed case studies—that assess the effectiveness of green‑infrastructure measures (e.g., tree canopy expansion, green roofs, cool pavements, urban wetlands) in reducing ambient and surface temperatures in midsize cities (population roughly 100,000–500,000). Relevant documents must present quantitative results, describe the measurement methodology, specify the climate zone or geographic context, and compare at least two mitigation strategies.

title: Remote Work Wellness
description: How does remote work affect employee mental health and what interventions can mitigate negative effects?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the mental health consequences of remote work, including stress, isolation, burnout, and anxiety, as documented in peer‑reviewed studies, industry reports, or reputable surveys. Relevant material should also describe evidence‑based interventions, policies, or best‑practice recommendations that organizations can adopt to support employee well‑being while working remotely.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state tax credits and rebates are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on financial incentives that homeowners can use to offset the cost of installing photovoltaic solar systems. Relevant material must include the federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC) details, any recent changes to its rate or eligibility, state‑level tax credits, cash rebates, utility‑company programs, and applicable deadlines or expiration dates for the 2023‑2024 period. Documents that merely mention solar energy without providing specific incentive information are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal and state incentives are available for residential solar panel installations in 2024?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date information on financial incentives, tax credits, rebates, and grant programs that homeowners can use to offset the cost of installing solar panels on a residence. Relevant documents should detail eligibility criteria, application procedures, monetary values, and any deadlines or limitations for both federal programs (e.g., Investment Tax Credit) and state‑specific incentives across the United States.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat islands affect energy consumption in residential buildings?
narrative: The user is seeking scholarly articles, government reports, and case studies that explore the relationship between the urban heat island effect and residential energy use. Relevant material should quantify temperature differentials, describe measurement techniques, analyze the impact on heating and cooling demand, and discuss mitigation strategies or policy recommendations.

title: Remote Work Migration
description: How has the rise of remote work since 2020 affected migration patterns to mid-sized U.S. cities?
narrative: The user seeks recent (2020 onward) studies, reports, or news articles that examine demographic shifts, housing market changes, and economic impacts in U.S. cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000, specifically attributing these trends to the increase in remote work. Relevant material must provide data, analysis, or expert commentary linking remote‑work adoption to people relocating to these mid-sized cities, and should not merely discuss remote work benefits unrelated to geographic movement.

title: Renewable Energy Financing
description: What innovative financing mechanisms are being used to fund large‑scale renewable energy projects in developing countries?
narrative: The user seeks recent (last five years) case studies, reports, or analyses that describe novel financing tools—such as green bonds, blended finance, climate funds, or public‑private partnerships—applied to utility‑scale renewable projects in low‑ and middle‑income nations. Relevant documents must detail the financing mechanism, participating stakeholders, project scale, and measurable outcomes or lessons learned.

title: Alpine Plant Phenology
description: How is climate change influencing the timing of flowering and leaf‑out in alpine plant species?
narrative: The user is seeking scholarly information that explains observed and projected shifts in phenological events (e.g., flowering, leaf‑out, seed set) of plants that grow at high elevations as a result of climate change. Relevant documents should present empirical data or model‑based analyses, preferably from peer‑reviewed journals, covering temperature or precipitation trends, altitude gradients, and species‑specific responses. Studies focusing on low‑land or non‑alpine ecosystems, purely descriptive travel guides, or unrelated health topics are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state tax credits and rebates are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on financial incentives that can reduce the cost of installing solar panels on a home. Relevant documents should list applicable federal tax credits (e.g., the Investment Tax Credit), state‑level tax credits, rebates, and any utility or local programs, including eligibility criteria, monetary amounts, expiration dates, and official sources. Materials that only discuss solar technology, general energy policy, or incentives that are no longer active are not relevant.

title: AI Governance
description: What comprehensive frameworks guide the ethical deployment and oversight of artificial intelligence in government agencies?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on policies, standards, and best‑practice models that public sector organizations can adopt to ensure AI systems are transparent, accountable, and ethically aligned. Relevant material includes governance structures, regulatory guidelines, risk‑assessment methodologies, case studies of government AI implementations, and scholarly analyses of AI ethics in the public domain. Documents that only discuss technical AI development, commercial industry standards, or non‑government contexts are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat island effects differ across cities of varying sizes and climate zones?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly articles, case studies, and governmental reports that examine the magnitude and drivers of urban heat island (UHI) phenomena in small, medium, and large cities across different climate zones. Relevant documents must provide quantitative measurements, comparative analyses, discussion of contributing factors (e.g., land‑use, building density, vegetation), and mitigation strategies or policy recommendations.

title: EU Renewable Subsidies
description: What economic effects have recent European Union renewable energy subsidies had on growth, employment, and electricity prices?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date (post‑2018) analyses, policy reports, or peer‑reviewed studies that assess the impact of EU subsidies for wind and solar power on macro‑economic indicators such as GDP growth, job creation, and retail electricity costs. Relevant material must provide quantitative evidence, cite reputable data sources, and focus specifically on subsidy schemes enacted by the EU or its member states.

title: Wheelchair Cost-Effectiveness
description: What are the long‑term cost and health outcome differences between powered and manual wheelchairs for elderly patients with chronic mobility limitations?
narrative: The user is seeking evidence‑based information that compares powered and manual wheelchairs in terms of total cost of ownership (purchase, maintenance, insurance/reimbursement), durability, and impact on health outcomes such as independence, fall risk, and quality of life for seniors with long‑term mobility impairments. Relevant documents include peer‑reviewed studies, systematic reviews, health economics analyses, clinical guidelines, and government or insurer policy documents that address cost‑benefit, reimbursement criteria, or comparative effectiveness. Documents that are merely product listings, rental advertisements, generic wheelchair descriptions, or unrelated therapeutic diet information are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Impact
description: What are the measurable effects of remote work on employee mental health across multinational corporations?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, industry reports, and case studies that evaluate mental‑health outcomes (e.g., stress, burnout, job satisfaction, anxiety) among employees who work remotely in multinational firms. Relevant sources should present quantitative or qualitative evidence, describe the study methodology, and consider factors such as geographic region, length of remote work, and any mitigation or support programs. Only documents that provide clear data or analysis from 2015 onward are considered relevant.

title: Offshore Wind Policy
description: What policy mechanisms have proven most effective at accelerating offshore wind farm deployment in Europe?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on governmental and regulatory approaches that have successfully increased offshore wind capacity in European countries. Relevant documents should discuss specific policies (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, auctions, tax incentives, permitting reforms), provide evidence of their impact on project timelines, investment levels, and electricity generation, and include recent case studies or comparative analyses across multiple nations. Materials that only mention offshore wind without addressing policy effectiveness, or that focus solely on technical engineering aspects, are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state tax credits and rebates are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks current, detailed information on financial incentives for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems, including the federal Investment Tax Credit percentage, state-specific tax credits, cash rebates, eligibility requirements, application processes, and any expiration dates. Relevant documents must explicitly describe these incentives, their monetary value or percentage, qualifying criteria, and procedural steps for claiming them.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What policy mechanisms have most effectively increased renewable energy adoption in European Union member states since 2010?
narrative: The user wants detailed information on specific governmental policies—such as subsidies, feed‑in tariffs, tax incentives, renewable portfolio standards, and regulatory frameworks—that have demonstrably boosted renewable energy capacity and investment in EU countries after 2010. Relevant documents must describe the policy, provide evidence of its impact (e.g., growth statistics, investment amounts, or case studies), and focus on the EU context. General overviews of renewable technologies or policies outside the EU are not considered relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state tax credits, rebates, and other incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on financial incentives that homeowners can use to offset the cost of installing solar panels. Relevant documents should detail the specific federal tax credit (e.g., Investment Tax Credit), state‑level tax credits or rebates, eligibility criteria, monetary amounts or percentage values, application processes, and any expiration dates. Sources that discuss only general solar benefits without concrete incentive data, or that are outdated (pre‑2022), are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Island
description: How do different urban design strategies mitigate the urban heat island effect in mid-sized cities?
narrative: The user wants detailed, up-to-date information on mitigation techniques for the urban heat island (UHI) phenomenon, emphasizing design interventions such as green roofs, reflective pavements, expanded tree canopy, and cool districts in cities with populations of 100,000–500,000. Relevant documents should explain the effectiveness, implementation challenges, and quantified temperature reductions of each strategy, provide case studies or comparative analyses, and be sourced from scholarly articles, technical reports, or government publications from the last 15 years.

title: Vinyl Collecting
description: What are the essential considerations for building, preserving, and valuing a collection of LP (long‑play) vinyl records?
narrative: The user seeks detailed guidance on acquiring, caring for, and assessing the worth of LP records, including information on pressing variations, condition grading scales, optimal storage conditions, cleaning methods, and current market trends. Relevant documents should address vinyl record formats, collector best practices, grading criteria, and resale platforms. Sources focusing on medical lumbar puncture, oral lichen planus, limited partnership law, gas‑container regulations, or bacterial lipopolysaccharides are not relevant.

title: Housing Cost Burden
description: What are the current guidelines and scholarly debates regarding the proportion of household income that should be allocated to housing costs in the United States?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive information on how much of a household's income is considered affordable for housing, including federal, state, and local policy thresholds, historical evolution of those thresholds, and recent academic discussions on the adequacy of the commonly cited 30% rule. Relevant documents should provide official guidelines, statistical analyses, and critical evaluations that address affordability metrics, variations by income level or region, and proposals for alternative measures.

title: Solar Incentive Programs
description: What federal and state incentives are available for residential solar panel installations in 2024?
narrative: The user seeks current (2024) information on financial incentives—tax credits, rebates, grants, and loan programs—provided by the U.S. federal government and individual states specifically for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant material must detail eligibility requirements, application steps, monetary amounts, deadlines, and any restrictions, while excluding content about commercial solar projects, non‑solar renewable technologies, or incentives that are no longer active.

title: Remote Work Migration
description: How has the rise of remote work influenced migration patterns to mid-sized U.S. cities since 2020?
narrative: The user is looking for recent, evidence‑based information on how remote‑work trends have affected population movement into U.S. cities with 100,000–500,000 residents. Relevant documents should provide quantitative data (e.g., census, ACS, housing market statistics), qualitative analyses, or case studies that discuss reasons for relocation (cost of living, quality of life, infrastructure) and cover the period from 2020 onward. Sources may include government reports, academic research, reputable news outlets, or industry analyses. Materials that focus solely on large metropolitan areas, pre‑2020 trends, or unrelated topics (e.g., lanolin, ginseng, Arkansas tourism) are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal, state, and local incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive, up-to-date information on financial incentives (tax credits, rebates, grants, and loan programs) that homeowners can use to offset the cost of installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents must detail the eligibility criteria, monetary value, application process, and geographic scope of each incentive, and should be dated within the last 12 months to reflect the most current policies.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal tax credits and state rebates are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date information on financial incentives that homeowners can claim when installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents must describe the federal Investment Tax Credit, any state‑specific rebate or tax credit programs, eligibility requirements, application processes, monetary values, and applicable deadlines, and should be current (2023‑2025).

title: Circular Packaging Policies
description: What are the current regulatory requirements and best‑practice guidelines for implementing circular (reusable or recyclable) packaging in the European Union?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date information on EU legislation, standards, and industry recommendations that govern circular packaging, including specific directives, compliance deadlines, labeling rules, and examples of companies successfully adopting such practices. Relevant documents should discuss legal texts, policy analyses, and practical implementation case studies, while excluding unrelated topics such as neuroscience, credit rating, or general waste management unrelated to packaging.

title: Airport Traffic Trends
description: What are the trends in international passenger traffic for the world's busiest airports over the past ten years, and what factors have driven those changes?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, up‑to‑date statistics and analysis of international passenger volumes for the top 20 busiest airports from 2015 through 2024. Relevant material must include yearly passenger counts, growth or decline percentages, seasonal patterns, and explanations such as new route openings, airline alliance impacts, infrastructure upgrades, policy changes, or external events like pandemics or construction projects. Documents that merely list airports without data, or that provide only anecdotal commentary, are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Storage
description: What are the most efficient and cost‑effective methods for storing energy generated from renewable sources?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date information on technologies that can store electricity from solar, wind, or other renewables. Relevant material should include recent (post‑2015) research papers, industry reports, or case studies that compare storage options such as lithium‑ion batteries, flow batteries, pumped hydro, compressed air, thermal storage, and emerging technologies. The focus is on efficiency, lifecycle cost, scalability, deployment examples, and environmental impact, with quantitative performance metrics and economic analyses.

title: Residential Solar Policies
description: What recent U.S. legislative measures have been introduced to promote residential solar photovoltaic installations?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date information on federal and state laws, regulations, tax credits, rebates, or other incentives enacted in the last three years that specifically target homeowners installing solar panels. Relevant documents must describe the policy name, enactment date, eligibility requirements, financial benefits, and implementation details. Materials that only discuss solar technology, general renewable‑energy policy, or commercial/utility‑scale solar without a residential focus are not relevant.

title: Carbon Capture Viability
description: What are the economic and technical factors influencing the commercial viability of large‑scale carbon capture and storage projects in the United States?
narrative: The user is researching the feasibility of deploying carbon capture and storage (CCS) at a utility‑scale in the US. Relevant documents should discuss recent cost estimates (e.g., $/ton CO2 captured), technology performance metrics (capture efficiency, energy penalty), policy incentives or tax credits, financing models, and case studies of operating or pilot CCS plants. Sources that focus only on unrelated topics such as water cooling, flammable gases, or concrete additives are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What strategies can cities implement to reduce the impact of urban heat islands on public health?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive information on practical measures—such as green roofs, street trees, reflective pavements, and related policies—that have been used or proposed to lessen urban heat island effects, including evidence of effectiveness, case studies, and guidance for implementation in different urban contexts.
